hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'Silanus' — 'Silanum' with -um is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'Silani' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has genitive in -is — 'Silani' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'Silani' ends in -i

steps.prompt: This is an indirect statement. What role does 'Silanum' play?

hints.direct object of 'video': In indirect discourse, the accusative is the subject of the infinitive — 'video' governs the whole clause
hints.object of the infinitive 'moveri': 'Moveri' is passive — 'Silanum' is its subject, not object
hints.ablative of agent: Ablative of agent would use 'a/ab' + ablative — 'Silanum' is accusative subject

hints.to move (active): 'To move' actively is 'movere' — 'moveri' is the present passive infinitive
hints.to have been moved: 'To have been moved' would be 'motum esse' — 'moveri' is present passive
hints.to remove: 'To remove' is 'removere' or 'amovere' — 'moveri' is passive of 'movere'

steps.prompt: What voice and tense is the infinitive 'moveri'?

hints.present active infinitive: Present active would be 'movere' — 'moveri' with -i ending is passive
hints.perfect passive infinitive: Perfect passive would be 'motum esse' — 'moveri' is present passive
hints.future passive infinitive: Future passive would be 'motum iri' — 'moveri' is present passive

hints.moneo, monere, monui, monitum: These are the parts of 'monere' (to warn) — similar but different verb
hints.maneo, manere, mansi, mansum: These are the parts of 'manere' (to remain)
hints.mordeo, mordere, momordi, morsum: These are the parts of 'mordere' (to bite)

hints.perfect indicative: Perfect would be 'vidi' — 'video' is present indicative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'videam' — 'video' is present indicative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'videbo' — 'video' is present indicative

hints.vinco, vincere, vici, victum: These are the parts of 'vincere' (to conquer)
hints.vito, vitare, vitavi, vitatum: These are the parts of 'vitare' (to avoid)
hints.vivo, vivere, vixi, victum: These are the parts of 'vivere' (to live)

translation: I see that Silanus is being deeply moved
